title: Testing Object Collaborations and Contracts · Program Defense
summary: Verify domain invariants, substitute implementations, event effects, persistence, and refactoring safety. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a contract-tested object system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test constructor and mutation invariants across valid boundaries, invalid inputs, and unchanged-state failures.
Test object collaboration through outcomes and role boundaries without over-specifying internal call order.
Choose real objects, fakes, stubs, spies, or mocks based on speed, determinism, behavior fidelity, and diagnostic value.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Oop Invariant Tests evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Oop Invariant Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Test constructor and mutation invariants across valid boundaries, invalid inputs, and unchanged-state failures. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python oop testing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Oop Invariant Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a contract-tested object system while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One happy-path constructor test proves an object remains valid after mutations. This fails the Oop Invariant Tests contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Descriptor Guide (Python 3.14.6) — Controls descriptor precedence, properties, functions, methods, and managed attribute implementation.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test constructor and mutation invariants across valid boundaries, invalid inputs, and unchanged-state failures.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Serialization Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify round-trip, version, missing-field, unknown-field, and invalid-data behavior at serialization boundaries.
